metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_262
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Flotillin-2 regulates in cooperation with Rab11a membrane trafficking processes in spines and participates in LTP-related AMPA receptor (GluA1) surface exposure in mouse hippocampal neurons.

13/4/2017 Pim
Fig.1 A-G
- shRNA KD of Reggie1 or reggie2 results in less spines --> regulation of dendritic spine organization
- Reggie1 KO mouse no effect on spine number, but less spine synapses --> regulation of synapse organization

Fig.1 G-J
- Reggie1 and Reggie2 localize to synaptic component transport vesicles in dendrites.

Fig.2 A,B
- RFP-Reggie1 is co-transported with PSD-95 in dendrites

Fig.2 C-Fig
- Reggie1 KD causes reduction in number of spine synapses (measured as juxtaposed presynaptic FM4-64FX and postsynaptic PSD-95-EGFP staining).

Fig.3
- Reggie1 KO cells show impaired transport of PSD-95, and reduced number of synapses (measured as juxtaposed synaptophysin/PSD-95 staining). Experimental description: Hippocampal neurons from 1 to 2 d old wt C57BL/6J and reggie-1 k.o. (Flot2-/-) mice. (p 32)
Reggie-1 knock-out (k.o.; Flot2 -/-) C57BL/6J mice were generously provided by Tak W. Mak (The Campbell Family Institute for Breast Cancer Research, University Toronto, Canada; Berger et al., 2013). K.o. and wild type (wt) C57BL/6J mice were raised in the animal facility, University of Konstanz. Male and female mice, 1-2 d and 2 months old, were used.

20/3/2017 Pim
- The species of overexpression constructs used in this paper are not relevant for species determination since they merely serve as specificity controls for KD/KO conditions.
- Species of RFP-reggie1 (Fig.2A,B) was traced back to **rat** origin based on PMID:17854803:
"Deletion mutants of reggie-1 were obtained by PCR using rat full-length reggie-1 cloned in pEGFP-N1 (Clontech, Heidelberg, Germany) (R1FL-EGFP)". This species does not prevent annotation to mouse species.
- Species of PSD-95-EGFP was traced to rat by email communication with David Brendt:

" 13 April 2017 19:34
This is rat.

David"
